summary: See clues file for full details. 10 tossups, 7 bonuses found.

works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 10 tossups and 7 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: An artist with this first name lived upstream from Alexander Pope's Twickenham villa and was Joseph Wright of Derby's teacher.
examples: An artist with this first name lived upstream from Alexander Pope's Twickenham villa and was Joseph Wright of Derby's teacher.

clues.clue: A 20-year-old artist with this first name created full-body portraits of the Viscountess Cremorne and Queen Charlotte.
examples: A 20-year-old artist with this first name created full-body portraits of the Viscountess Cremorne and Queen Charlotte.

clues.clue: In 1921, the Grosvenor family sold a painting by an artist with this first name, falsely thought to have been a response to the (*) Eighth Discourse to the Royal Academy.
examples: In 1921, the Grosvenor family sold a painting by an artist with this first name, falsely thought to have been a response to the (*) Eighth Discourse to the Royal Academy.

clues.clue: In 2021, a Kehinde Wiley painting replaced a portrait of Sarah Moulton by an artist with this first name.
examples: In 2021, a Kehinde Wiley painting replaced a portrait of Sarah Moulton by an artist with this first name.

clues.clue: That portrait at the Huntington was opposite a portrait by another artist with this first name, whose subject, Jonathan Buttall, holds a feathered cap and wears the title color.
examples: That portrait at the Huntington was opposite a portrait by another artist with this first name, whose subject, Jonathan Buttall, holds a feathered cap and wears the title color.

clues.clue: One of these artists broke with the other and began exhibiting at Schomberg House after his portrait Three Eldest Princesses was hung "above the line." The artistic quotation used by one of these artists was mocked by an artist who supported the other in a painting where prints are burned by the title Conjuror.
examples: One of these artists broke with the other and began exhibiting at Schomberg House after his portrait Three Eldest Princesses was hung "above the line." The artistic quotation used by one of these artists was mocked by an artist who supported the other in a painting where prints are burned by the title Conjuror.

clues.clue: One of these artists called the other's late work "fancy pictures" but praised his Girl with Pigs.
examples: One of these artists called the other's late work "fancy pictures" but praised his Girl with Pigs.

clues.clue: One of these artists said "Confound the nose, there's no end to it!" of a model the other painted between figures holding a dagger and poisoned chalice.
examples: One of these artists said "Confound the nose, there's no end to it!" of a model the other painted between figures holding a dagger and poisoned chalice.

clues.clue: A myth claims that the color theory in one of these artist's Eighth Discourse caused the other to paint a portrait of Jonathan Buttall.
examples: A myth claims that the color theory in one of these artist's Eighth Discourse caused the other to paint a portrait of Jonathan Buttall.

clues.clue: It's not Fragonard, but a 2017 biography of this artist by James Hamilton identifies a phallic bag in a painting by him, whose subjects are shown "without their heads" in a 1998 work by Yinka Shonibare.
examples: It's not Fragonard, but a 2017 biography of this artist by James Hamilton identifies a phallic bag in a painting by him, whose subjects are shown "without their heads" in a 1998 work by Yinka Shonibare.

clues.clue: This artist, who once claimed that he was "sick of portraits" and wanted to play the viol, painted his friend Carl Friedrich Abel with a viola da gamba.
examples: This artist, who once claimed that he was "sick of portraits" and wanted to play the viol, painted his friend Carl Friedrich Abel with a viola da gamba.

clues.clue: A Pomeranian sheepdog tugs at a dress in a portrait by this artist in which the Halletts walk in a park.
examples: A Pomeranian sheepdog tugs at a dress in a portrait by this artist in which the Halletts walk in a park.
